title: Obstacle-triggered safety stop
_about: Reusable incident rule. The compiler (tools/incident_spec.py) evaluates this against an incident's timeline/logs and EMITS the annotations shape that tools/incident_rules.py already consumes. Signals declare HOW to find/observe evidence in the data; anchor times (t) are discovered by the compiler via anchor_strategy, never written here. 0.2 adds real-compatible compilation: search_window_strategy (event-derived window), required: if_available (a corroborating source a robot may not have), and positive_required (a conclusion asserting an obstacle needs at least one positive observation). The deterministic verifier is unchanged.

requires._note: Documentation only (no code consumer). optional_metrics mirrors required: if_available below.

lidar_front_zone._note: front-sector min LaserScan range (geometric, no ML), emitted by the exporter. A state signal ALWAYS emits one evidence: label=obstacle if it crosses inside the search window, else label=clear (a real negative observation). The clear observation is what makes sensor disagreement 'conflicting' rather than 'missing'.

velocity_halt._note: event/crossing signal: emits evidence only when it fires; absence -> required_present false -> low. after:stop_event avoids matching an unrelated early zero. abs: a halt means |v| <= threshold — a signed velocity in reverse must not satisfy it.

conclusions.statement: The robot stopped because an obstacle entered the demo safety zone ahead.
conclusions._search_window_note: Declared fallback window. When search_window_strategy finds its anchor event in the logs, the window is derived from the event time instead; callers may still override.
conclusions._required_note: if_available: a second independent distance source that some robots simply do not have. Absent from every timeline row -> demoted to a label-less corroboration member (verifier caps at medium); present but not firing stays a real negative observation.
conclusions._positive_required_note: At least one zone member must observe 'obstacle'; all-clear sensors + stop event + halt must NOT support this conclusion (the compiler degrades the placeholder signal to out-of-window -> low).

expected._about: The correctness oracle: ONE obstacle-stop spec evaluated against three synthetic datasets must yield three verdicts (matches current eval). Not per-scenario specs.
